sourceScope.primarySourceFile: AG 22 - Interpretation Regarding Non-Forfeiture Values for Policies With Indeterminate Premiums.pdf
sourceScope.sourceTitle: AG 22 - Interpretation Regarding Non-Forfeiture Values for Policies With Indeterminate Premiums

observedSectionWindows.note: Title, adjusted-premium rule text, and background information on the indeterminate-premium / maximum-gross-premium comparison.

boundaries: Keep planning focused on this single AG 22 PDF.
Do not widen into AG 23 or later guideline files.
Keep the active guideline posture explicit in every batch artifact.
Keep the page image as the backstop for exact wording because the text layer is noisy / encoded.
Treat the guideline as review-only nonforfeiture guidance, not learner-facing policy text.
Keep the batch source-bound and review-only.
exclusions: No eLearning scripts, slides, quizzes, or app exports.
No raw source files in Git.
No other guideline files in the same wave.

topicMap.label: Nonforfeiture values for policies with indeterminate premiums
crossReferenceWatchlist: Standard Nonforfeiture Law for Life Insurance
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep this slice within page 1 and do not absorb later guideline files.

proposedBatches.title: One-page AG 22 indeterminate-premium guideline

pageTarget.notes: Capture the full one-page guideline as a single review-only slice and stop at the page boundary.

proposedBatches.rationale: Capture the full one-page guideline as a single review-only slice and stop at the page boundary.

reviewStandards.regulatoryRequirement: Treat express duty, permitted treatment, or required nonforfeiture-value language in the indeterminate-premium context as the requirement; preserve the citation and source context.
reviewStandards.definitionOrTerminology: Treat indeterminate premium policies, adjusted premiums, gross premiums, maximum gross premiums, and nonforfeiture values as terminology when the text explains rather than directs.
reviewStandards.reserveMethodStructure: Treat the greater-of comparison and annual adjusted-premium logic as structure until the text clearly instructs a separate duty.
reviewStandards.calculationStructure: Treat the way the guideline frames the nonforfeiture comparison as calculation structure until the text clearly instructs a separate duty.
reviewStandards.formulaContext: Treat the gross-premium schedules and maximum-gross-premium language as context until the text clearly instructs a calculation step.
reviewStandards.crossReferenceMapping: Treat references to the Standard Nonforfeiture Law for Life Insurance, Section 5, Section 5-c, Section 6, or related premium schedules as mapping notes unless operational text is included.
reviewStandards.backgroundContent: Treat the title line and framing sentence as background content unless they impose a duty.
reviewStandards.boundaryControlWindow: Treat the one-batch page window as a review-only boundary that should not be merged forward.
reviewStandards.requiresHumanInterpretation: Treat the indexing choice and the noisy OCR layer as requiring human actuarial interpretation unless the text is explicit.
reviewStandards.sourceQualityCaveat: Treat the page-image wording as the backstop and do not widen the batch because OCR looks uncertain.

appReady: export is sanitized, stable, and versioned
ragReady: review status no longer requires human disposition

currentChecksNeeded: Plan file exists and parses as JSON.
The AG 22 plan markdown exists and carries the expected headings.
Every proposed batch is review-only by default.
The plan keeps the scope to page 1 only.
The source posture is explicitly active.
Each planned batch has a matching batch definition.
The AG 22 batch definition file stays synchronized with the plan.
futureChecksSuggested: One-page / short-guideline checks for future compact guideline files.
No learner-facing, app-ready, or RAG-ready promotion checks.
Source-bound nonforfeiture-context checks once extraction starts.
scriptImplications: scripts/ag22-batch-definitions.mjs should stay synchronized with the planned batch-100 slice when extraction is authorized.
scripts/batch-definitions.mjs should import the AG 22 definition file so the shared runner can find the planned batch ID.
scripts/validate-scaffold.mjs should verify that the AG 22 plan file exists, parses cleanly, and preserves the review-only posture.
The shared runner should continue to honor the same stop conditions used for the earlier guideline batches.

notes: Controlled AG 22 planning artifact. Planned batch 100 remains review-only by default and is not an extraction run.
